problem: My school's Physics Club has 22 members.  It needs to select 3 officers: chairman, vice-chairman, and sergeant-at-arms.  Each person can hold at most one office.  Two of the members, Penelope and Quentin, will only be officers if the other one is also an officer.  (In other words, either both Penelope and Quentin are officers, or neither are.)  In how many ways can the club choose its officers?

classified_questions.question: What is the problem asking us to find?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many officers does the club need to select?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many members are in the club?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can each person hold more than one office?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What are the three officer positions that need to be filled?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Are there any restrictions in selecting the officers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Who are the members that have a special condition for being officers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many total ways can the club select officers if Penelope and Quentin cannot be officers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many total ways can the club select officers if both Penelope and Quentin must be officers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain how you arrived at the final answer of 6960?
classified_questions.label: b
